pi_items.metadata_value: A complete beginner's guide to getting started with WordPress, including features, benefits, and frequently asked questions.
pi_items.verified_value: A complete beginner's guide to getting started with WordPress, including features, benefits, and frequently asked questions.
pi_items.source: Task description directly instructs the agent to set this exact excerpt string.

The setup_wordpress.sh script creates the post with title "10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs" without an explicit slug. The default WordPress sanitize_title behavior converts this title to "10-essential-wordpress-plugins-every-site-needs", not the truncated version claimed in metadata.

metadata_value: Do I need to know coding to use WordPress?
What is the difference between WordPress.org and WordPress.com?
verified_value: Do I need to know coding to use WordPress?
What is the difference between WordPress.org and WordPress.com?
pi_items.source: Task description directly provides these exact questions as instructions.

metadata_value: Yes, the core WordPress software is 100% free and open-source.
No, you can build a complete website without writing any code using themes and plugins.
WordPress.org is the self-hosted software, while WordPress.com is a hosting service.
verified_value: Yes, the core WordPress software is 100% free and open-source.
No, you can build a complete website without writing any code using themes and plugins.
WordPress.org is the self-hosted software, while WordPress.com is a hosting service.
pi_items.source: Task description directly provides these exact answers as instructions.

privileged_info_summary: The WordPress FAQ metadata claims exactly match the task description instructions for the excerpt, questions, and answers. However, the expected_internal_link_slug claim "10-essential-wordpress-plugins" contradicts the environment setup scripts. The linked target post is dynamically created with the title "10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs" via WP-CLI without a specified slug, which naturally generates "10-essential-wordpress-plugins-every-site-needs" under default WordPress slug rules.
